Changes from 2.0 to 2.1
=======================

Getting Started
* New `BOFS init` CLI wizard that walks users through creating a new BOFS project, and can launch the project with the browser opened automatically.
* `SECRET_KEY` is no longer required in the config; an auto-generated hash is created and stored when the database is initialized.
* Major overhaul of [the documentation](https://docs.frankensystem.net/), including expanded config options reference, util function reference, blueprint guidance, and docs for the `title` attribute, `required_selection`, and the `init` command.

New Question Types and Questionnaire Features
* Added a "video" question type for embedding video stimuli.
* Added a "picture_select" question type for choosing one option from a set of images. Supports horizontal (default) or vertical centered layouts, an explicit per-question `width`, and an `auto_resize` flag that normalizes every image to the smallest natural dimension in the group. The data type of the stored column is auto-detected (integer / float / string) from the image values, and the `images` list is validated at startup.
* Checklist now supports options with associated text fields.
* Radiolist now supports an "other" option with a free-text field, and can require a specific option to be chosen in order to continue.
* Radiogrid can now be made optional, supports `questions` as an alias for `q_text`, and provides better validation feedback.
* Questions can now define a `title` attribute that displays above the question box.
* Questions can be configured to show only for specific conditions.
* Questionnaire questions are now loaded server-side instead of via htmx.
* More thorough questionnaire validation: any issues are reported on the console and the user is directed to a friendlier error page.
* If a participant returns to a prior questionnaire (not currently exposed in the UI, but supported internally), their previously-entered values are now reloaded into the form.

Conditions
* Can now optionally load conditions from a `.csv` file or an existing `.db` file, making longitudinal studies easier to set up.
* Can "pause" individual conditions to stop new participants from being assigned to them.
* If all conditions are paused, new participants are blocked from entering the study entirely.

Admin Panel
* New procedure diagram that visualizes the page flow.
* Can now preview an individual participant's responses.
* Admin panel now redirects users back to the log-in page when their session is no longer valid.
* Refined admin control panel shown at the bottom of pages when logged in as admin or running in debug mode.
* Export button on the database table view moved to the top of the page.

Debug Mode and Developer Experience
* In debug mode, a condition picker is shown so developers can choose which condition to test.
* In debug mode (or when accessed via localhost), users are now offered the option to restart the experiment from `/end`; the restart route keeps the admin login session intact.
* The debug toolbar is now shown on pages before the participant exists, with buttons disabled and a note explaining that consent must be given (or the participant created) first.
* A console warning is shown when `@verify_correct_page` is missing on a custom route; a new `@suppress_activity_polling` decorator silences the warning when the polling script can't be injected.

Custom Routes and Templates
* Activity polling is now injected via `after_request`, so participants on long-running tasks with fully custom HTML are no longer falsely marked as abandoned. Pages are also marked "submitted" when the session's current URL is updated.
* Participant progress tracking moved entirely out of `@verify_correct_page` into a `before_request_` handler, so custom routes that omit the decorator still track progress properly.
* Can now set the template's header colour from the config file.
* Can load alternative `style.css` files via configuration.
* Template macros extracted into their own file for easier reuse and overriding.
* `mTurkID` can now also be passed in via the `external_id` URL argument.
* Allow `APPLICATION_ROOT` to be missing from the config.

Expression Engine and Show-If
* Added a small expression engine for `participant_calculations`, replacing the previous use of `eval()`.
* Added `show_if` predicates on questionnaire questions (live, browser-side, against same-page form values) and on entries in `PAGE_LIST` (server-side, against stored answers). See `docs/advanced/expressions.rst` for the syntax.
* Page-level `show_if` can reference `condition` (the participant's assigned condition number) and `tables.<name>.<column>` (per-participant aggregates from a JSONTable's `exports` block).
* Each arm of `conditional_routing` now accepts an optional `show_if` predicate alongside (or instead of) `condition`. The first arm whose `condition` matches and whose `show_if` is true wins.
* Validation now rejects Python keywords as field IDs, calculated-field names, table column names, and table export field names. It also flags collisions where a table's export field name matches a column on the same table, and warns when a page-level `show_if` references a `group_by` export (which produces multiple values per participant). The names `condition` and `tables` are reserved at the top of an expression and cannot be used as questionnaire/table filenames, field IDs, or calculated-field names.

Participant Data Access in Templates and Custom Routes
* `participant` is now available as a template variable in every BOFS template; resolves to `None` when no participant is in session (admin previews, error pages).
* New `participant.evaluate(expression)` runs a BOFS expression against the participant's stored data. The same syntax used in `show_if` and `participant_calculations` is now usable from templates and custom blueprint code.
* New `participant.has_questionnaire(name, tag='')` distinguishes a real submission from the blank-default row that `participant.questionnaire(name)` returns when nothing has been submitted yet.
* `participant.table(name)` now returns a `TableAccessor` exposing the participant's raw rows (`accessor.rows`), every scalar export aggregate as an attribute (`accessor.<export_field>`, computed lazily and memoised per accessor instance), and all aggregates as a dict (`accessor.exports`). **Breaking**: the previous return value was the SQLAlchemy backref list directly. The accessor proxies iteration, `len()`, indexing, `bool()`, and `in` to `accessor.rows`, so `{% for row in participant.table('foo') %}`, `participant.table('foo')|length`, and `participant.table('foo')[0]` continue to work without changes. Code that called list-only methods (`.append`, `.sort`) on the return value will break — operate on `accessor.rows` instead.

Internal Refactoring
* Introduced a service layer to consolidate previously scattered logic: `ParticipantService`, `SessionRecoveryService`, `ParticipantRoutingService`, `ParticipantQuestionnaireService`, and `AdminStatsService`.
* `Results` class moved to `services/data_export.py`; fixed an issue with export flags discovered during the move.
* Added unit tests covering JSON tables, built-in model classes, results calculations, session management, page routing, participant management, and questionnaire handling.

Bug Fixes
* Fixed `redirect_next_page` edge cases.
* Fixed condition being lost when external ID was loaded via URL arguments.
* Fixed debug condition picker showing on projects with no conditions.
* Fixed slider default size not applying to the right label.
* Fixed page-list composition issue in the admin progress route.
* Fixed Python 3.9 typehint compatibility issue.
* Fallback source for standard deviation/variance now uses the population method.

Changes from 1.2 to 2.0
=======================

Core Changes
* Removed support for Python 2.7.x
* Changes to support the lastest Flask version and latest SQLAlchemy.
* [New documentation](https://docs.frankensystem.net/).
* Updated all JS libraries.
* Consent text is now read in from a `consent.html` file next to the configuration file instead of from the configuration file.
* Added "BOFS" command to start projects instead of needing a separate `run.py` file.
* No more need for the "app" directory to be a part of the project.
* Use `flask-compress` to serve brotli and gz files (e.g., for Unity WebGL builds).
* Improved auto-reload feature that now works with templates.
* Export code rewritten to minimize the chance of throwing exceptions.
* Simple error messages will now show up if in production mode, rather than a generic 500 error.
* If not in debug mode, save any 500 errors to a log file.
* When project stats, check for missing DB columns from tables and questionnaires and add them (only works with SQLite).
* Detect web scrapers and if found exclude them from the condition counts and progress page.
* Many bug fixes.

Project Configuration Options
* Can now specify a specific completion code, or redirect users to an external URL upon reading `/end`.
* `/start_mturk` route is now `/external_id`.
* Can now configure what the prompt on `/external_id` says.
* If `PROLIFIC_PID` is passed in as a URL parameter, the session variable is set and the input field on `/external_id` is automatically populated with the value passed.
* Conditions are now defined with labels and can be disabled selectively; labels show on progress page and are used in the export instead of the numbers.
* Can now configure `ABANDONED_MINUTES` and `COUNTS_INCLUDE_ABANDONED`, relating to ignoring participants who abandoned the task when considering participant counts within conditions.

Administration
* Can now download SQLite database from admin panel as well as clear it out.
* Admin panel styling changes.
* Admin login now redirects user to where they were trying to go instead of always redirecting to the progress page.
* Added the ability to selectively indicate individual participants to exclude from the participant counts.
* Added a results page to the admin panel to view summary statistics of any numeric data.

Tables, Questionnaires, and Blueprints
* Can define custom exports within the [JSON-defined tables](https://docs.frankensystem.net/en/latest/tables.html).
* Can now use 'HAVING' clauses in custom exports.
* Can now configure default value of sliders.
* Some slight improvements to how the questionnaires are rendered on mobile devices.
* `/table` POST route now understands JSON data.
* Blueprints no longer need a `views.py` file to be included.
* Can now have ["simple" html pages](https://docs.frankensystem.net/en/latest/simple_pages.html), that support templates but can be shown without any additional Python code.
* It is now possible to define [custom question types](https://docs.frankensystem.net/en/latest/questionnaires/custom_questions.html) to use within questionnaires.
* Can put questionnaire and tables inside of blueprint folders
